steps.title: Spot the causal factor
steps.content: Replacing ELF3 eliminated flowering acceleration at 27°C but left basic flowering at 22°C unchanged. This directly implicates ELF3 as the enabling agent.

steps.title: Connect ELF3 to the response
steps.content: Choice C states that ELF3 enables A. thaliana to respond to increased temperatures. When ELF3 is swapped for a stiff brome version, the acceleration disappears—confirming ELF3's causal role. The other three either overstate uniqueness, speculate about untested production mechanisms, or misread what the temperatures reveal. That matches choice C — C is correct.
